identity.name: NurtureNote
identity.description: An educational nutrition reference for expectant mothers, prioritizing safety and clarity without offering medical advice.

typography.pairing_strategy: The 'Old Money Tech' variant: Trustworthy Serif for headings to convey authority and tradition, paired with a soft Rounded Sans (Nunito) for body text to feel approachable and nurturing.

usage.gradients: No strict gradients. Use soft, organic mesh gradients for backgrounds if needed: 'bg-gradient-to-br from-[#F7F5F2] to-[#E8E6E1]'.
usage.text_contrast: Ensure all text is #2D3748 (Charcoal) or darker on light backgrounds. Avoid pure black #000000.

textures.grain: Use a subtle SVG noise overlay with 2-3% opacity on the main background to reduce digital harshness.

layout_strategies.grid_system: Mobile-first. Single column for mobile, max-width-2xl centered for desktop content. Use 'The Bento Grid' for the home dashboard.
layout_strategies.spacing: Generous. Use 'p-6' or 'p-8' for cards. 'gap-6' for lists. Avoid cramping.

containers.main: max-w-md mx-auto min-h-screen bg-[#FDFCF8] shadow-2xl overflow-hidden border-x border-slate-100

DisclaimerModal.description: Full-screen modal on first load. Cannot be dismissed without action.
DisclaimerModal.style: bg-[#FDFCF8] fixed inset-0 z-50 flex flex-col justify-center px-8
elements: Body: Clear text stating non-medical nature.

FoodCard.description: Card displaying food item and safety status.
FoodCard.style: bg-white rounded-3xl p-6 shadow-card border border-slate-100 relative overflow-hidden group

SafetyBadge.style: px-4 py-1.5 rounded-full text-sm font-bold uppercase tracking-wider inline-flex items-center gap-2

ReferenceBlock.description: Section at bottom of details citing sources.
ReferenceBlock.style: bg-slate-50 rounded-xl p-4 text-xs text-slate-500 mt-8 border-t border-slate-100

hero.desc: Use for home page or welcome screen.

food_healthy.desc: Use for food search or category headers.

doctor.desc: Use for 'When to seek care' or 'About' section.

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. THEME: Implement a 'Light' theme only. Dark mode is optional/secondary for this specific persona (trust/medical clarity).
2. DISCLAIMERS: Implement the disclaimer logic FIRST. The app must not function without user acknowledgement.
3. LANGUAGE: Refactor all hard-coded strings to be soft/educational. Change 'Forbidden' to 'Generally Recommended to Avoid'.
4. COMPONENTS: Use existing shadcn components but override their styles with the radius and shadow values defined above.
5. ROUTING: Ensure the Disclaimer Modal acts as a gate before the Main Layout is accessible.
6. Q&A GUARD: Add regex/logic to detect symptom keywords (bleeding, pain, fever) and return the canned safety response immediately.
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur).
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: transition: all. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN
